Cornell University Department of Natural Resources records
New York State College of Agriculture and Life Sciences. Department of Natural Resources
Hartz, Wayne.
21-18-991
31.5 cubic feet. 31.5 cubic feet.
1909-1995. Reports, bulletins, field notes, slides, radio talks, publications, photographs, drawings, and subject files. Includes annual reports, monthly reports, bulletins, plans of work, field notes, slides of Cornell campus and activities, radio talks, publications, photographs, committee records, drawings, subject files, and other materials of the Cornell University Department of Natural Resources, formerly the Conservation Department. Contact Information:
Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections 2B Carl A. Kroch Library Cornell University Ithaca, NY 14853 (607) 255-3530 Fax: (607) 255-9524 rareref@cornell.edu http://rmc.library.cornell.edu
Compiled by:
C. RoteGuy Smith
Date completed:
April 1974February 2008
EAD encoding:
Ann Hubert, November 2003Evan Fay Earle, February 2008
Date modified:
RMC Staff, December 2014
Cite As:
Cornell University Department of Natural Resources records, #21-18-991. Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections, Cornell University Library.
Scope and content
Includes annual reports, monthly reports, bulletins, plans of work, field notes, slides of Cornell campus and activities, radio talks, publications, photographs, committee records, drawings, subject files, and other materials of the Cornell University Department of Natural Resources, formerly the Conservation Department. Also, 1939 report on land use in Arnot Forest and slides and photographs used in a 1983 master's thesis by Wayne Hartz.
Scope and content
Photograph album scrapbook documenting the history of the department, its faculty, buildings, students, and activities, including maple syrup production and the Cornell Biological Field Station at Shackelton Point, Bridgeport, N.Y. Includes photographs, newsletters, and articles
Photographs. Drawings. Maple syrup -- New York (State) Conservation of natural resources. Cornell University. Conservation Department Cornell University. Arnot Forest Cornell University Biological Field Station
